summary: Most likely cause is a runtime latency regression in checkout-service. p95 latency increased 4.8x and TimeoutException errors increased 12x vs baseline, with the Lambda function identified as the dominant latency segment.
evidenceHighlights: p95 latency increased 4.8x vs baseline (3200ms vs 670ms)
TimeoutException errors increased 12x vs baseline (243 vs 20)
Dominant segment in sampled checkout-service traces is checkout-service Lambda (100% of sampled segment time)

confidenceExplanation.whyRankedHighest: Deterministic ranking selected this hypothesis by confidence gap (78% vs 16%; delta 62%) with 3 strongest supporting evidence item(s).

strongestEvidence.summary: p95 latency increased 4.8x vs baseline (3200ms vs 670ms)
X-Ray response time for checkout-service changed 4.8x vs baseline
TimeoutException errors increased 12x vs baseline (243 vs 20)

aiIdentified: DynamoDB item schema and recent table changes

ai.reason: Latency spike and dominant Lambda segment in traces align strongly with a runtime regression.

exceptionSamples: TimeoutException: upstream call to payments-service exceeded 5000ms (req-id: abc123)

traceDependencyHint.summary: Faulted downstream call in sampled checkout-service traces: dynamodb.get_item (fault, 6/10 traces)

operatorFocus.whereToLookFirst: Start with checkout-service logs — exception errors present; then correlate with checkout-service metrics in the incident window.

Error log signals are present but weaker than the latency evidence. Resource: checkout-service.

aiNextBestActions.action: Inspect Lambda function duration and memory metrics during the incident window
aiNextBestActions.why: Lambda is the dominant latency segment; duration spike may indicate a timeout or resource exhaustion.
aiNextBestActions.expectedSignal: Duration approaching or exceeding configured timeout, or memory utilisation spike

aiNextBestActions.action: Inspect dependency timeout distribution in trace/log samples
